question: Describe the support structure of the steam generator tubes.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary side corrosion risks considered low.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) contains two steam generators (SGs) integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The steam generator system (SGS) includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs. The SG tubes and associated welds are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, ensuring separation between the primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s systems to optimize performance across various power levels. Key operational parameters—such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and water levels—are established and verified using both software and manual calculations, accounting for uncertainties and design requirements. The system is designed for low pressure losses to support natural circulation, and uncertainties related to steam generator tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in nuclear facility applications.

edges.description: Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms of steam generator tubes, specifically wear due to flow-induced vibration and tube-to-support plate interaction, while Section B provides a general overview of the steam generator's placement and integration within the reactor pressure vessel, thus Section A provides specific details that elaborate on the general description in Section B regarding the steam generator's internal support structure.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms in steam generator (SG) tubes, specifically wear due to flow-induced vibration and foreign objects, while Section B provides a general system evaluation context for SG operation and references further SG description in Section 5.4, thus Section A provides specific details that elaborate on the general SG description in Section B.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms of steam generator tubes and references existing program guidelines, while Section B lists specific industry standards and guidelines (such as EPRI and ASME documents) that underpin those program guidelines and design practices.

Section A provides a general description of the steam generator's placement and associated components within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B references Section 5.4 (where Section A is located) for further details about the steam generators, indicating that B points to A for specific information.

Section A describes the components within the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, while Section B lists references that provide standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and quality assurance of reactor coolant boundary components such as steam generator tubes.

Section A references Section B by citing external standards and guidelines (such as EPRI and ASME documents) that are relevant to the evaluation and integrity of the steam generator support structure.
